name: Chilli Pepper

book_claims.claim: May play a role in prevention of obesity by activating TRPV1 and AMPK pathways
book_claims.mechanism: Capsaicin and capsaicinoids activate TRPV1 and AMPK molecular pathways. Inhibits inflammation in fat and liver cells, improves glucose control in animal studies.

book_claims.claim: Capsinoid supplementation associated with abdominal fat loss and increased energy expenditure
book_claims.mechanism: Capsinoids enhance heat-producing activity of brown adipose tissue, increasing energy expenditure over 6 weeks

supplementary_research.finding: Large prospective cohort study (>485,000 Chinese adults, 7-year follow-up) found regular spicy food consumption (chilli peppers) associated with 14% reduced all-cause mortality compared to eating spicy food less than once per week.

Meta-analysis found capsaicin consumption increased energy expenditure by approximately 50 kcal/day and reduced appetite. Effects were modest but consistent across studies, supporting the book's obesity prevention claims.

Capsinoid supplementation (6 mg/day for 6 weeks) increased cold-induced thermogenesis and brown adipose tissue activity in humans, confirming the book's claims about brown fat activation and energy expenditure.
Moderate evidence for modest increases in metabolic rate and fat oxidation. Strong evidence for pain relief (topical). Capsaicin desensitises TRPV1 pain receptors with repeated use.

culinary_pairings.source: Universal pairing across Sichuan, Thai, Italian, and Mexican cuisines
Complementary heat compounds in curries and stir-fries
Aglio, olio e peperoncino -- the ultimate minimalist pasta
Foundational heat-spice pairing in chili con carne and curries

seasonality: Fresh chillies peak in summer and early autumn. Dried chillies available year-round with excellent shelf life. Hundreds of varieties from mild (Anaheim) to extreme (Carolina Reaper). Sweet peppers contain similar but non-pungent compounds (capsinoids).

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: 0.1-6 mg (varies enormously by variety; habanero ~16 mg)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Rapidly absorbed. Activates TRPV1 receptors. Repeated exposure causes receptor desensitisation (tolerance). Lipophilic -- pair with fats.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Present in sweet peppers (CH-19 Sweet variety)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Same metabolic effects as capsaicin without the burn. Activate brown adipose tissue at 6 mg/day.
Fresh chillies are among the richest sources of vitamin C. Degrades significantly with drying.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: 534 mcg RAE (59% RDA, fresh red chilli)
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Red and orange varieties are particularly rich. Fat-soluble; enhanced by cooking in oil.

synergies.description: Heat and pungency amplify each other across many cuisines
Both activate anti-inflammatory pathways; combined in many curry blends
